message:
feat(terminal,render,docs): add multi-row brain visualization with emoji mode, interactive navigation controls, and auto-follow behavior

This commit significantly enhances the terminal brain visualization with multi-row display, emoji mode support, and interactive navigation controls for exploring brain activations across different agents and layers. Additionally, it implements intelligent auto-follow behavior in the GUI for smoother agent tracking workflows.

Terminal multi-row brain visualization (scriptbots-app/src/terminal/mod.rs):

Enhanced activation display from single-row to multi-row scrollable view:
- Now displays 3 rows of brain activations simultaneously (previously 1 row) for richer neural state visualization
- Added activation_row_offset field to support vertical scrolling through activation layers
- Row display adapts to available terminal width: emoji mode shows 16 columns, ASCII mode shows 32 columns
- Each row shows a horizontal slice through the activation grid at indices [row_index * width .. (row_index + 1) * width]

Emoji mode brain visualization:
- Detects emoji-capable terminals via palette.is_emoji() and terminal width > 40
- Maps activation values to expressive emoji symbols: 🔥 (>0.85), 🌶 (>0.6), ✨ (>0.35), · (>0.15), space (≤0.15)
- Provides more intuitive "hot" vs "cold" neuron visualization compared to block characters
- Falls back to ASCII block characters (█▆▅▃▂▁) in narrow terminals or when emoji disabled
- Emoji mode particularly effective for quickly identifying highly-active neurons at a glance

Why emoji thresholds differ from ASCII:
- Emoji mode uses 5 levels (>0.85, >0.6, >0.35, >0.15, else space) vs ASCII 6 levels (>0.8, >0.6, >0.4, >0.2, >0.1, else ▁)
- Emoji symbols have stronger visual weight, so fewer gradations prevent cluttered appearance
- Fire emoji (🔥) reserved for very high activations (>0.85) to emphasize peak neural activity
- Space character for near-zero allows visual "breathing room" in emoji mode

Interactive navigation controls:
- `[` and `]` keys cycle through brain layers (activation_layer_index), enabling inspection of multi-layer networks like NeuroFlow
- ↑ and ↓ arrow keys page through activation rows (activation_row_offset), scrolling vertically through large activation grids
- ← and → arrow keys navigate between agents (focused_agent_cursor wraps around agent count), comparing brain states across population
- All navigation keys update display immediately without requiring pause or tick advancement

Implementation of focused agent selection:
- Modified refresh_snapshot to pull brain activations from the agent at index (focused_agent_cursor % agent_count)
- Uses world.agents().iter_handles().nth(idx) to select the idx-th agent from the population
- Fetches brain_activations from the selected agent's runtime (world.runtime().get(agent_id))
- Changed Snapshot::from_world to set brain_activations: None by default, now exclusively populated in refresh_snapshot based on user focus
- This architecture allows terminal to display any agent's brain state on demand without recomputing all activations

Why cursor wrapping:
- focused_agent_cursor % agent_count ensures cursor always points to valid agent even as population fluctuates
- When population shrinks (deaths), cursor automatically wraps to valid range without manual bounds checking
- When navigating forward past last agent, wraps to first agent; backward from first wraps to last
- Provides seamless navigation experience during dynamic population changes

Multi-row layout logic:
- Computes start_row = activation_row_offset.min(layer.height.saturating_sub(1)) to clamp offset within valid range
- Shows rows_to_show = 3.min(layer.height - start_row) rows, preventing overrun when near grid bottom
- Each row spans start + cols elements in the flattened values vector
- Label "Brain " appears only on first row; subsequent rows indented with "      " for visual grouping

Added derives and helpers:
- BrainLayerView now derives Clone and Debug for better integration with snapshot system
- Snapshot struct modified to handle brain_activations via refresh path rather than construction path
- snapshot.brain_activations_layer_indexed(index) added to fetch specific layer by index (assumed to be implemented based on usage)

Help panel documentation updates:
- Added three new help entries documenting brain navigation controls:
  * "[ / ]    Cycle brain layers (console view)"
  * "↑ / ↓    Page brain heatmap rows (console view)"
  * "← / →    Change focused agent (console view)"
- Clarifies these controls are specific to console/terminal view (not GUI)
- Positioned after expanded panels toggle (x) for logical grouping of view manipulation controls

GUI auto-follow behavior (scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs):

Implemented intelligent camera follow mode triggered by mouse clicks:
- Single-click (no Shift) on agent: automatically enables FollowMode::Selected to track the clicked agent
- Single-click (no Shift) on empty space: disables follow mode (FollowMode::Off) when previously following
- Shift-click behavior unchanged: extends selection without affecting follow mode
- Provides intuitive "click to track, click away to stop" workflow for observing specific agents

Technical implementation:
- Snapshots focused agent state BEFORE mutating selection (had_focus_before flag)
- Calls update_selection_from_point to perform selection logic (may focus agent or clear focus)
- Snapshots focused agent state AFTER selection update (has_focus_after flag)
- If focus gained (has_focus_after && !had_focus_before), enables FollowMode::Selected
- If focus lost (had_focus_before && !has_focus_after), disables follow mode
- Only executes auto-follow logic when extend=false (non-Shift clicks)

Why this improves UX:
- Previously: users had to manually enable follow mode via menu/hotkey after selecting agent
- Now: single click both selects and follows, reducing workflow from 2 actions to 1
- Clicking empty space acts as intuitive "stop following" gesture
- Shift-extend behavior preserved for power users building multi-agent selections
- Natural spatial interaction: "I clicked this agent, so track it" vs "I clicked away, so stop tracking"

Edge cases handled:
- Shift-click never triggers auto-follow, preventing unexpected camera movement during multi-selection
- Follow state only changes when focus state changes (no redundant follow mode updates)
- Empty-space click only disables follow if was previously following (had_focus_before check)
- Works correctly with existing follow modes (Selected, Oldest, Youngest) - only manipulates Selected mode

Documentation (README.md):

Added screenshot location documentation:
- Clarified that terminal screenshots (saved via `S` key) write to `screenshots/frame_<tick>.txt`
- Previously undocumented; users had to search filesystem to find saved frames
- Format includes tick number in filename for easy chronological sorting and correlation with simulation state
- Frames saved in plain text format suitable for sharing, diffing, and archiving

Integration benefits:
- Terminal brain visualization now on par with GUI for exploratory analysis of neural network behavior
- Users can navigate through NeuroFlow's multi-layer networks in terminal without requiring GUI
- Agent comparison workflows enabled: press → repeatedly to scan population for interesting brain patterns
- Auto-follow reduces GUI friction for common "select and track" use case
- Documentation completeness improved for terminal screenshot feature

Future extension points:
- Multi-row display could show variable row counts based on terminal height
- Emoji mode could support additional symbols for more granular intensity levels
- Layer cycling could display layer names/indices in status bar
- Agent cursor could display agent ID/generation in status panel
- Auto-follow could extend to other follow modes (Oldest/Youngest) with modifier keys

Testing recommendations:
- Terminal: launch with MLP or NeuroFlow brain, press ←→ to cycle agents, [ ] to cycle layers, ↑↓ to scroll rows
- Emoji mode: enable with `e` key on wide terminal (>40 cols), verify 🔥🌶✨ symbols appear
- GUI: click agent to verify camera follows, click empty space to verify follow stops
- Screenshot: press `S` in terminal, verify frame written to screenshots/frame_<tick>.txt

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -516,7 +516,7 @@ An emoji-rich terminal renderer is planned behind a `terminal` feature/CLI mode
   - If emojis render as tofu/misaligned, install an emoji-capable font (e.g., Noto Color Emoji) or toggle off with `e`.
 - Narrow symbols mode: press `n` to switch to width-1 friendly symbols while keeping emoji colors off-background; helpful for strict terminals/alignment.
 
-Keybinds: space (pause), +/- (speed), s (single-step), b (toggle metrics baseline), S (save ASCII screenshot), e (emoji), n (narrow symbols), x (expanded panels), ?/h (help), q/Esc (quit). The terminal HUD shows tick/agents/births/deaths/energy, Insights (rolling metrics), Mortality panel, Brains leaderboard, recent events log, and an emoji world mini-map. The layout is responsive and auto-expands panels on wider terminals; press `x` to toggle.
+Keybinds: space (pause), +/- (speed), s (single-step), b (toggle metrics baseline), S (save ASCII screenshot), e (emoji), n (narrow symbols), x (expanded panels), ?/h (help), q/Esc (quit). The terminal HUD shows tick/agents/births/deaths/energy, Insights (rolling metrics), Mortality panel, Brains leaderboard, recent events log, and an emoji world mini-map. The layout is responsive and auto-expands panels on wider terminals; press `x` to toggle. Screenshots saved via `S` are written under `screenshots/frame_<tick>.txt`.
 
 ## Storage & analytics
 - DuckDB schema (`ticks`, `metrics`, `events`, `agents`) with buffered writes and maintenance (`optimize`, `VACUUM`).
